description: Add MCP resources and prompts so any AI client connected to the OpenTabs MCP server gets comprehensive, AI-optimized documentation. Currently, AI clients only get a 75-line SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS string and 1 prompt (build_plugin). After this work, the server exposes static resources (guides, references) via the MCP resources capability, a dynamic status resource, two new prompts (troubleshoot, setup_plugin), and enhanced instructions that tell AI clients about the available resources.

userStories.title: Add MCP resources capability and handler infrastructure
userStories.description: Register the MCP resources capability on the server and add handlers for resources/list and resources/read. Create the infrastructure for serving static text resources from a new mcp-resources.ts module.
acceptanceCriteria: Server capabilities object includes `resources: {}` alongside existing tools, logging, and prompts capabilities
ListResourcesRequestSchema handler is registered and returns an array of resource definitions
ReadResourceRequestSchema handler is registered and returns resource content by URI
Resources are defined in a new mcp-resources.ts file with a RESOURCES array pattern matching the existing PROMPTS array in mcp-prompts.ts
Each resource has uri, name, description, and mimeType fields
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup.ts (add resource handlers + capability)
- platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-resources.ts (new file — resource definitions and resolver)

Context:
The server currently uses the low-level `Server` class from `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/index.js` (not McpServer). Capabilities are declared in `createMcpServer` at line 337:
```typescript
capabilities: {
  tools: { listChanged: true },
  logging: {},
  prompts: { listChanged: true },
},
```

Handlers are registered in `registerMcpHandlers` (lines 179-246) using `server.setRequestHandler(schema, handler)`. The schemas come from `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/types.js`.

The MCP SDK exports `ListResourcesRequestSchema` and `ReadResourceRequestSchema` from `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/types.js`. The response format for list is `{ resources: [{ uri, name, description?, mimeType? }] }`. For read: `{ contents: [{ uri, mimeType?, text }] }`.

Changes:
1. Add `ListResourcesRequestSchema` and `ReadResourceRequestSchema` to the imports from `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/types.js` (line 24-29)
2. Add `resources: {}` to the capabilities object (line 337). No need for `subscribe` or `listChanged` since resources are static.
3. Register two new handlers in `registerMcpHandlers`:
   - `ListResourcesRequestSchema` → returns `{ resources: getAllResources(state) }`
   - `ReadResourceRequestSchema` → extracts `uri` from params, calls `resolveResource(state, uri)`, returns `{ contents: [{ uri, mimeType: 'text/markdown', text }] }` or error
4. Create `platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-resources.ts` with:
   - A `ResourceDefinition` interface: `{ uri: string; name: string; description: string; mimeType: string }`
   - A `RESOURCES` array of static resource definitions (just the metadata, not content yet — content comes in later stories)
   - A `getAllResources(state)` function that returns the RESOURCES array
   - A `resolveResource(state, uri)` function that returns `{ uri, mimeType, text }` or null
   - Export both functions

The RESOURCES array should define these URIs (content will be added in subsequent stories):
- `opentabs://guide/quick-start`
- `opentabs://guide/plugin-development`
- `opentabs://guide/troubleshooting`
- `opentabs://reference/sdk-api`
- `opentabs://reference/cli`
- `opentabs://reference/browser-tools`
- `opentabs://status` (this one is dynamic — resolveResource should build it from ServerState)

For now, static resources can return a placeholder string. The dynamic `opentabs://status` resource should return a JSON summary built from `state` (similar to the health endpoint at `platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts` lines 321-353 but simplified for AI consumption).

Follow the exact same code patterns as mcp-prompts.ts — a RESOURCE_MAP for O(1) lookup, clean separation of definition vs resolution.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the McpServerInstance interface — it only needs setRequestHandler which already accepts any schema
- Do NOT modify existing prompt or tool handlers
- The resources capability should NOT include subscribe or listChanged (resources are static or computed on read)
- Use `text/markdown` as mimeType for all guide/reference resources, `application/json` for the status resource

userStories.title: Write the quick-start guide resource content
userStories.description: Write the opentabs://guide/quick-start resource content — an AI-optimized guide that helps any AI client understand how to use the OpenTabs platform for normal users.
acceptanceCriteria: resolveResource returns non-empty markdown content for opentabs://guide/quick-start

Content covers: what OpenTabs is, how to install (npm), how to start the server, how to load the Chrome extension, how to configure MCP clients (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf), how to install a plugin, and how to make the first tool call
Content is written for AI consumption — structured with clear headers, code blocks, and actionable steps
Content mentions the plugin review flow and permission system
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-resources.ts (add content for quick-start resource)

Context:
This resource targets the 'normal user' actor — someone whose AI client is connected to OpenTabs and wants to understand how to use it. The AI client will fetch this resource to learn how to guide the user through setup and usage.

The content should be written in Markdown, optimized for AI processing (not human readability on a website). Key differences from the human docs:
- Direct, information-dense prose — no hand-holding tone
- Structured data over narrative — use tables, lists, code blocks
- Include exact command examples with expected output
- Include the MCP client configuration JSON for each supported client
- Mention what tools are available (browser tools, plugin tools, extension tools)
- Explain the permission model concisely (off/ask/auto, skipPermissions)

Source of truth for content:
- docs/content/docs/quick-start.mdx (human version to adapt)
- docs/content/docs/install/index.mdx (installation details)
- The SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS string in mcp-setup.ts lines 252-326 (already covers some of this)
- docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx lines 560-633 (MCP client config JSON)

The content should be a string constant in mcp-resources.ts, returned by resolveResource when uri matches. Keep the content comprehensive but not excessively long — aim for 150-250 lines of markdown.

Constraints:
- Do NOT duplicate the full SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS content — reference that it exists and covers security
- Do NOT list every browser tool — mention the count (40) and key categories
- Content must be accurate — use the actual CLI commands from the codebase

userStories.title: Write the plugin development guide resource content
userStories.description: Write the opentabs://guide/plugin-development resource — a comprehensive AI-optimized guide for building OpenTabs plugins, covering the SDK, patterns, and conventions.
acceptanceCriteria: resolveResource returns non-empty markdown content for opentabs://guide/plugin-development

Content covers: plugin architecture (browser page context), OpenTabsPlugin base class, defineTool factory, SDK utilities (fetch, DOM, storage, timing, logging), ToolError factories, Zod schema rules, build/test workflow, publishing
Content includes exact code patterns (plugin class, tool file, API wrapper) that an AI can use directly
Content mentions the isReady() polling pattern and auth extraction strategies
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-resources.ts (add content for plugin-development resource)

Context:
This resource targets the 'plugin developer' actor. An AI client connected to OpenTabs fetches this to understand how to build a new plugin. This overlaps significantly with the existing `build_plugin` prompt in mcp-prompts.ts, but the resource is a reference document (pull-based, read when needed) while the prompt is a task workflow (invoked to execute a specific task).

Source of truth:
- platform/plugin-sdk/CLAUDE.md
- platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts (exports, ToolDefinition interface)
- platform/plugin-sdk/src/plugin.ts (OpenTabsPlugin class)
- platform/plugin-sdk/src/sdk.ts (SDK utilities)
- platform/plugin-sdk/src/errors.ts (ToolError)
- docs/content/docs/guides/plugin-development.mdx (human version)
- docs/content/docs/sdk/tools.mdx, utilities.mdx, lifecycle-hooks.mdx, error-types.mdx
- The existing build_plugin prompt in mcp-prompts.ts lines 101-447 (has good patterns to extract)

Key content sections:
1. Architecture overview (plugin code runs in browser, not server)
2. OpenTabsPlugin abstract class (name, displayName, description, urlPatterns, tools, isReady)
3. defineTool factory (name, displayName, description, summary, icon, group, input, output, handle)
4. SDK utilities reference table (all functions with signatures)
5. ToolError factories (auth, notFound, rateLimited, validation, timeout, internal)
6. Zod schema rules (no transform, no pipe, refine safety, describe every field)
7. Lifecycle hooks (onActivate, onDeactivate, onNavigate, onToolInvocationStart/End, teardown)
8. Build and test workflow (opentabs-plugin build, plugin_list_tabs)
9. Publishing to npm (package.json opentabs field, npm publish)
10. Common patterns (auth extraction, API wrapper, globalThis token caching)

Aim for 300-400 lines. Dense reference format, not tutorial style.

Constraints:
- Include ACTUAL SDK utility function signatures from the source code
- Include ACTUAL ToolError factory signatures
- Do NOT just copy the human docs — restructure for AI consumption

userStories.title: Write the troubleshooting guide and SDK/CLI/browser-tools reference resources
userStories.description: Write content for the remaining 4 static resources: troubleshooting guide, SDK API reference, CLI reference, and browser tools reference.
acceptanceCriteria: resolveResource returns non-empty content for opentabs://guide/troubleshooting
resolveResource returns non-empty content for opentabs://reference/sdk-api
resolveResource returns non-empty content for opentabs://reference/cli
resolveResource returns non-empty content for opentabs://reference/browser-tools
Troubleshooting covers: extension not connected, tab closed/unavailable, plugin not reviewed, permission denied, timeout, rate limiting, tool not found — with specific resolution steps for each
SDK API reference lists all SDK utility functions with type signatures
CLI reference covers all opentabs and opentabs-plugin commands
Browser tools reference lists all 40 browser tools organized by category with descriptions
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-resources.ts (add content for 4 resources)

Context:
These are the remaining static reference resources. Each should be a dense, AI-optimized markdown document.

### opentabs://guide/troubleshooting
Source: docs/content/docs/reference/troubleshooting.mdx + the error handling section of SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS (lines 318-326)
Format: Problem → Cause → Resolution table. Include the exact error messages AI clients will see.
Key issues:
- Extension not connected (WebSocket down)
- Tab closed / Tab unavailable (plugin loaded but no matching tab)
- Plugin not reviewed (permission 'off', review flow needed)
- Permission denied (user rejected approval)
- Dispatch timeout (30s default, progress resets)
- Rate limiting (retryAfterMs)
- Tool not found (plugin not installed)
- Concurrent dispatch limit (5 per plugin)

### opentabs://reference/sdk-api
Source: platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts (all exports), sdk.ts (utilities), errors.ts (ToolError)
Format: Function signatures in TypeScript, organized by category (DOM, Fetch, Storage, Page State, Timing, Logging, Errors)
Key utilities to document:
- DOM: waitForSelector, querySelectorAll, querySelector, getTextContent, getInnerHTML
- Fetch: fetchJSON, postJSON, postForm, postFormData, putJSON, patchJSON, deleteJSON
- Storage: getLocalStorage, setLocalStorage, removeLocalStorage, getSessionStorage, setSessionStorage, removeSessionStorage, getCookies
- Page: getPageGlobal, getCurrentUrl, getDocumentTitle
- Timing: retry, sleep, waitUntil
- Logging: log.debug, log.info, log.warn, log.error
- Errors: ToolError.auth, .notFound, .rateLimited, .validation, .timeout, .internal

### opentabs://reference/cli
Source: platform/cli/src/commands/ (all command files), docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx
Format: Command → Usage → Description. Include subcommands.
Commands: start, stop, status, audit, doctor, logs, update, plugin (search/create/list/install/remove), config (show/set/path/reset/rotate-secret)
IMPORTANT: Use the CORRECT config set key formats:
- tool-permission.<plugin>.<tool> (value: off|ask|auto)
- plugin-permission.<plugin> (value: off|ask|auto)
- port, localPlugins.add, localPlugins.remove

### opentabs://reference/browser-tools
Source: platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/index.ts (all 40 tools), @opentabs-dev/shared BROWSER_TOOLS_CATALOG
Format: Organized by category with tool name, description, and key parameters.
Categories: Tab Management, Content Retrieval, DOM Interaction, Scroll & Navigation, Storage & Cookies, Network Capture, Console, Resource Inspection, Site Analysis, Extension Diagnostics

Constraints:
- Keep each resource focused — not too long (100-200 lines each)
- Reference resources should be tables/lists, not prose
- Use accurate information from the actual source code, not the (potentially stale) human docs

userStories.title: Implement dynamic opentabs://status resource from server state
userStories.description: The opentabs://status resource should return a real-time JSON snapshot of the server state — plugins loaded, extension connected, tab states, permissions, tool counts. Built from ServerState, similar to the /health endpoint but AI-optimized.
acceptanceCriteria: resolveResource for opentabs://status returns valid JSON (mimeType application/json)
JSON includes: extensionConnected, plugins (name, toolCount, tabState, tabs), browserToolCount, pluginToolCount, skipPermissions, uptime
JSON includes per-plugin tab info (tabId, url, title, ready) when extension is connected
JSON does NOT include sensitive data (secrets, auth tokens, full adapter source)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-resources.ts (implement dynamic status resource)

Context:
The status resource is the only dynamic resource — it reads from ServerState at request time. The /health endpoint already serializes most of what we need (platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts lines 270-354), but the status resource should be simpler and AI-focused.

The resolveResource function already has access to ServerState (passed from the handler in mcp-setup.ts). For the status URI, build the JSON from state:

```typescript
const buildStatusResource = (state: ServerState): string => {
  const plugins = [...state.registry.plugins.values()].map(p => ({
    name: p.name,
    displayName: p.displayName,
    toolCount: p.tools.length,
    tools: p.tools.map(t => `${p.name}_${t.name}`),
    tabState: state.tabMapping.get(p.name)?.state ?? 'closed',
    tabs: (state.tabMapping.get(p.name)?.tabs ?? []).map(t => ({
      tabId: t.tabId, url: t.url, title: t.title, ready: t.ready,
    })),
  }));
  return JSON.stringify({
    extensionConnected: state.extensionWs !== null,
    plugins,
    failedPlugins: [...state.registry.failures],
    browserToolCount: state.cachedBrowserTools.length,
    pluginToolCount: [...state.registry.plugins.values()].reduce((sum, p) => sum + p.tools.length, 0),
    skipPermissions: state.skipPermissions,
    uptime: Math.round((Date.now() - state.startedAt) / 1000),
  }, null, 2);
};
```

Key state fields to use:
- `state.extensionWs !== null` → extensionConnected
- `state.registry.plugins` → plugin list (Map<string, RegisteredPlugin>)
- `state.tabMapping` → per-plugin tab state (Map<string, TabMapping>)
- `state.registry.failures` → failed plugins
- `state.cachedBrowserTools.length` → browser tool count
- `state.skipPermissions` → permission bypass flag
- `state.startedAt` → uptime calculation

Constraints:
- Do NOT include secrets (state.wsSecret), adapter IIFE source (state.registry.plugins[].iife), or raw audit entries
- Do NOT include the full health endpoint payload — keep it focused on what an AI agent needs to know
- Return JSON with 2-space indentation for readability

userStories.title: Add troubleshoot and setup_plugin MCP prompts
userStories.description: Add two new MCP prompts: 'troubleshoot' for guided debugging workflows and 'setup_plugin' for installing and configuring an existing plugin from npm.
acceptanceCriteria: prompts/list returns 3 prompts: build_plugin, troubleshoot, setup_plugin
troubleshoot prompt accepts optional 'error' argument (the error message to diagnose)
troubleshoot prompt returns a structured debugging workflow with specific tool calls to make
setup_plugin prompt accepts 'name' argument (plugin npm package name)
setup_plugin prompt returns a step-by-step workflow: install, configure, review, test
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-prompts.ts (add 2 new prompts)

Context:
The existing prompts system is in mcp-prompts.ts. The PROMPTS array (line 39) contains definitions, PROMPT_MAP provides O(1) lookup, and resolvePrompt dispatches to resolver functions. Follow the exact same patterns.

### troubleshoot prompt
Arguments:
- `error` (optional): The error message or symptom the user is experiencing

The resolved prompt should be a user-role message with a structured debugging workflow:
1. Check extension connectivity: `extension_get_state` → verify WebSocket connected
2. Check plugin state: `plugin_list_tabs` → verify plugin loaded and tab ready
3. If error mentions 'not reviewed': walk through the review flow (plugin_inspect → review → plugin_mark_reviewed)
4. If error mentions 'Extension not connected': guide user to check Chrome extension is loaded, check chrome://extensions, reload extension
5. If error mentions 'timeout': check if tool reports progress, suggest increasing timeout or checking network
6. If error mentions 'permission' or 'denied': explain the permission model, suggest config set commands
7. Check server logs: `extension_get_logs` for adapter injection issues
8. Check browser console: `browser_enable_network_capture` + `browser_get_console_logs` for page errors

Include the specific tool calls to make at each step. The prompt should be actionable — an AI reading it should know exactly what tools to call.

### setup_plugin prompt
Arguments:
- `name` (required): Plugin name or npm package name (e.g., 'slack' or '@opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-slack')

The resolved prompt should walk through:
1. Search for the plugin: `opentabs plugin search <name>` (CLI) or describe the install command
2. Install: `opentabs plugin install <package-name>`
3. Open the target web app in a browser tab
4. Verify plugin loaded: `plugin_list_tabs(plugin: '<name>')`
5. Review the plugin: explain the review flow (plugin_inspect → review → plugin_mark_reviewed)
6. Test a tool: suggest calling a read-only tool to verify everything works
7. Configure permissions: explain how to set plugin-permission and tool-permission via CLI

Constraints:
- Follow the exact code patterns in mcp-prompts.ts (PROMPTS array, PROMPT_MAP, resolvePrompt switch, resolver functions)
- Each prompt's resolved text should be 80-150 lines — detailed but focused
- Use the correct CLI commands (tool-permission.<plugin>.<tool> NOT the stale format)

userStories.title: Enhance SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS to reference available resources and prompts
userStories.description: Update the SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS string to tell AI clients about the available MCP resources and prompts they can fetch for deeper context. Currently, instructions only cover tool categories, security, and error handling.
acceptanceCriteria: SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS includes a section about available resources with their URIs and descriptions
SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS includes a section about available prompts and when to use them
Instructions advise AI clients to fetch opentabs://status as a first step to understand the current state
Instructions advise fetching guide resources when the user asks about setup, plugin development, or troubleshooting
The total instructions length stays under 6000 characters to avoid excessive system prompt bloat
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup.ts (update SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS at lines 252-326)

Context:
The SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS string (lines 252-326) is sent to MCP clients in the initialize handshake. It's injected into the AI's system prompt automatically. Currently ~75 lines covering tool categories, security rules, review flow, multi-tab targeting, permissions, and errors.

The instructions should NOT dump all resource content (that defeats the purpose of pull-based resources). Instead, tell AI clients:
1. Resources are available via MCP resources/read — fetch them when you need deeper context
2. List the available resources with 1-line descriptions
3. Recommend fetching opentabs://status first to understand the server's current state
4. Mention the 3 prompts (build_plugin, troubleshoot, setup_plugin) and when to use each

Add a new section after the existing 'Error Handling' section:

```
## Available Resources

Fetch these via MCP resources/read when you need detailed guidance:

- `opentabs://guide/quick-start` — Installation, configuration, and first tool call
- `opentabs://guide/plugin-development` — Full guide to building OpenTabs plugins (SDK, patterns, conventions)
- `opentabs://guide/troubleshooting` — Common errors and resolution steps
- `opentabs://reference/sdk-api` — Plugin SDK API reference (utilities, errors, lifecycle hooks)
- `opentabs://reference/cli` — CLI command reference (opentabs, opentabs-plugin)
- `opentabs://reference/browser-tools` — All 40 browser tools organized by category
- `opentabs://status` — Live server state: loaded plugins, extension connectivity, tab states

Fetch `opentabs://status` first to understand the current server state before troubleshooting.

## Available Prompts

- `build_plugin(url, name?)` — Step-by-step workflow for building a new plugin for a web app
- `troubleshoot(error?)` — Guided debugging when tools fail or the platform misbehaves
- `setup_plugin(name)` — Install, configure, review, and test an existing plugin from npm
```

Constraints:
- Keep the additions concise — 20-30 lines max for the two new sections
- Do NOT remove or modify any existing sections (security rules, tool categories, etc.)
- Do NOT dump resource content into instructions — just list URIs with 1-line descriptions
- The total SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS string should stay under ~120 lines / 6000 characters
